summary: sensivity is a covert GTA V / FiveM game cheat with persistence, host fingerprinting, and obfuscated payload
details: On require()/launch, launcher.js mutates the Windows registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run with an entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that runs a bundled VBS script (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) which respawns node.exe launcher.js — impersonating Microsoft OneDrive's updater for stealth. The launcher additionally sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (a real Windows system process name) and spawns a detached, windowsHide:true supervisor (SENSIVITY_SUPERVISOR=1) that respawns a worker on every exit and PowerShell-kills any other process listening on TCP:3000. launcher.js then eval()s a 140KB heavily obfuscated server.obf.js (obfuscator.io-style: 682-entry rotated RC4 string array, control-flow flattening, ~914 decoder-wrapper calls) inline in the launcher process. The decoded payload reads HKLM\...\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell, os.hostname(), and the system volume's UniqueId/serial, SHA-256s the concatenation into an HWID, and POSTs it with a license key and nonce to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint (host begins 'iz...'). The package loads sens.node, a 6.85MB native Windows addon containing player-skeleton bone strings ('LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head'), 'Teleport', 'License Accepted', etc.; server.obf.js enumerates processes matching 'GTAProcess*' and 'FiveM*' and exposes start/stop/setConfig/performAction over a socket.io server on:3000 to manipulate the GTA V / FiveM client. launcher.js also polls Chrome/Edge/Brave/Opera/Opera_GX every 3s and hides the cheat UI unless a 'YouTube' window title is detected — anti-streamer evasion. Installer harm: persistent autostart with a Microsoft-impersonating name, host-identifier exfiltration to an undocumented endpoint, eval of an obfuscated remote-code-equivalent blob at module load, native game-process tampering that risks anti-cheat (EAC/BattlEye) bans on the installer's account, and a network listener on:3000 with RPC over socket.io.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
